scenarios.title: Save a durable checkpoint before ending the session
scenarios.prompt: I am switching to another task. Save a checkpoint for this repo so I can resume later without rereading the whole conversation.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Leaves a loose summary in chat or suggests relying on session memory without creating a durable repo-local note.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Creates a new append-only checkpoint under `.codex/checkpoints/`, captures branch plus remaining work, and reports the saved path.

scenarios.title: Resume from the most relevant recent checkpoint
scenarios.prompt: What was I working on here? Resume from the latest checkpoint for this branch and tell me the next step.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Guesses from recent chat context or gives a generic answer without reading durable checkpoint state.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Reads the latest checkpoint for the current branch, summarizes the prior work, and gives a compact next-step resume summary.

scenarios.title: Branch mismatch should warn but not block resume
scenarios.prompt: Resume the latest checkpoint even if it came from a different branch. I just need to understand where I left off.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Either ignores branch context completely or refuses to resume because the branch is different.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Reads the latest checkpoint, warns about the branch mismatch, and still provides a usable resume summary.

scenarios.title: Plan Mode save should not pretend to write files
scenarios.prompt: You are in Plan Mode. Save a checkpoint for this planning session before I leave.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends it can write the checkpoint immediately even though the session is still in Plan Mode.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Explains that real save execution belongs in Default mode, tells the user to use Shift+Tab, and still leaves a compact checkpoint draft in the response.
